channel.title: Forma Blog — Train smart. Think deep.
channel.description: Science-backed writing on HRV, recovery and adaptive endurance training.

item.title: How Slow Do I Need to Run for Zone 2? The Complete Guide
item.description: Discover the precise pacing guidelines for effective Zone 2 training, from lactate threshold testing to conversational pace markers.

item.title: How Does My Garmin Watch Know That I Am Stressed
item.description: Discover the science behind Garmin's stress monitoring: HRV algorithms, physiological markers, and how your watch translates data into stress scores.

item.title: How Hard is it to Get a 100 Sleep Score in Garmin?
item.description: Getting a perfect 100 sleep score on Garmin requires understanding how the algorithm weighs duration, deep sleep, and recovery metrics.

item.title: Why Is My Garmin Training Readiness Always Low?
item.description: Your Garmin training readiness stays low due to HRV baselines, sleep debt, and algorithm sensitivity. Learn what's really happening and how to respond.

item.title: How Fast Does Zone 2 Pace Improve? Real Timeline Data
item.description: Zone 2 pace improvements follow predictable patterns. Learn the realistic timelines, what drives adaptation speed, and why consistency matters more than intensity.

item.title: How to Train to Improve HRV: Science-Based Strategies
item.description: Learn evidence-based training methods to improve heart rate variability. Master polarized training, recovery protocols, and HRV optimization techniques.

item.title: What Garmin Recovery Metrics Actually Mean — And When to Ignore Them
item.description: Garmin's Body Battery, recovery time, and HRV status look precise — but what do they actually measure? A science-backed breakdown for endurance athletes.

item.title: How Sleep Debt Destroys Endurance Performance — and the Numbers Behind It
item.description: Sleep debt silently erodes VO2max, glycogen repletion, and pacing discipline. Here's what the research says and how to protect your training gains.

item.title: Cycling Training Zones: Why Your FTP Isn't Everything
item.description: FTP is just one number. Learn why cycling training zones matter more, how to use them effectively, and what metrics beyond FTP tell you about your real fitness.

item.title: Why Your Easy Runs Are Probably Too Hard (And It's Killing Your Gains)
item.description: Most runners train too hard on easy days, blunting adaptation and accumulating fatigue. Learn what easy really means and why Zone 2 is your biggest performance lever.

item.title: The Science of Adaptive Training for Endurance Athletes
item.description: Discover how adaptive training uses HRV, sleep and recovery signals to optimise every workout. Why physiology-driven plans outperform fixed schedules.

item.title: Training Load Management: ATL, CTL and the ACWR Explained
item.description: ATL, CTL and ACWR explained for runners and cyclists. Learn how managing your acute-to-chronic workload ratio reduces injury risk and builds peak fitness.

item.title: Garmin vs Oura vs WHOOP: Which Wearable Data Should You Trust?
item.description: Garmin, Oura and WHOOP compared for endurance athletes. Which wearable gives the most reliable HRV and recovery data? What the numbers actually mean.

item.title: The Athlete's Paradox: Why Training More Doesn't Mean Performing Better
item.description: More miles, more intervals — but better results? The science behind why recovery builds fitness, and why the best athletes train less than you think.
